title: Fixing a Node.js Express Middleware Route with Incorrect Route Parameter Validation

statement.markdown: A Node.js Express application is built to handle user requests to different routes. One of the routes is designed to handle GET requests to a specific path with a parameter. However, the current implementation does not properly validate the type of the parameter, leading to unexpected behavior when the user sends a request with an invalid parameter type.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a string representing the route path, and the parameter name will be provided in the request.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return a boolean indicating whether the request was successful or not.
statement.notes: The function should handle both valid and invalid input types.

examples.explanation: Valid input with a numeric parameter
Invalid input with a string parameter
Invalid input with a numeric parameter

hints.text: First hint text: Use a regular expression to validate the parameter type.

editorial.title: Editorial title: Validating Route Parameters in Express
editorial.intuition: Explanation of the correct approach: Use a function to validate the parameter type and then check the route path to ensure it matches the expected pattern.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach summary: Write a function to manually check each character in the route path and parameter to ensure they match the expected type.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a regular expression to match the route path and parameter pattern.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution details: Use a function to validate the parameter type and then check the route path to ensure it matches the expected pattern.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Correctness proof: Verify that the function returns the correct output for both valid and invalid input types.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: Test the function with different input types and verify the output.
commonMistakes: Mistake 1: Not validating the parameter type
Mistake 2: Not checking the route path

starterCode.javascript: function validateRoute(route, param) { // your code here }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def validateRoute(self, route, param): pass

buggyStarterCode.javascript: function validateRoute(route, param) { let regex = /:/; if (regex.test(route)) { return true; } else { return false; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def validateRoute(self, route, param): return '/:' in route

functionSignature.javascript: function validateRoute(route, param) { // your code here }
